title: Bus

shortDialogue.speaker: 지용
shortDialogue.korean: 잘 지냈어? 요즘 뭐 하고 지내?
shortDialogue.english: How are you doing? What are you up to these days?

longDialogue.speaker: 지용
longDialogue.english: Oh, Su-hyeon. It's been a while.

longDialogue.speaker: 수현
longDialogue.english: Oh? Ji-yong. It's been a really long time!

longDialogue.speaker: 지용
longDialogue.korean: 잘 지냈어? 요즘 뭐 하고 지내?
longDialogue.english: How are you doing? What are you up to these days?

longDialogue.speaker: 수현
longDialogue.korean: 나 아직 졸업 못 했어. 지용아, 미안한데 나 버스 왔어.
longDialogue.english: I still can't graduate. Ji-yong, I'm sorry, but my bus is coming now.

longDialogue.speaker: 지용
longDialogue.english: What number bus do you take?

longDialogue.speaker: 지용
longDialogue.korean: 나도 그거 타. 잘했다. 같이 타자.
longDialogue.english: I'm taking that, too. That's great. Let's take it together.

longDialogue.speaker: 지용
longDialogue.english: Do you usually take this bus?

longDialogue.speaker: 지용
longDialogue.english: I am meeting a friend in Hongdae.

longDialogue.speaker: 수현
longDialogue.english: Oh, that's right. This bus goes to Hongdae.

longDialogue.speaker: 지용
longDialogue.korean: 응. 나이 버스 자주 타. 자주 봐자.
longDialogue.english: Yeah. I take this bus often. I'll see you often, then.

culturalTip: When you take public transportation in Korea and use a transportation card, you can get a discount if you transfer between buses or between a bus and the subway within 30 minutes after you disembark. At night, you can get a discount if you transfer within one hour. Even if you have to transfer many times during your journey, it is not expensive to do so.
koreanReview:
- Short Dialogue
지용: 잘 지냈어? 요즘 뭐 하고 지내?
수현: 나 아직 졸업 못 했어.

- Long Dialogue
지용: 어, 수현아! 오랜만이다!
수현: 어? 지용아. 진짜 오랜만이다!
지용: 잘 지냈어? 요즘 뭐 하고 지내?
수현: 나 아직 졸업 못 했어. 지용아, 미안한데 나 버스 왔어.
지용: 너 몇 번 타?
수현: 6716번.
지용: 나도 그거 타. 잘했다. 같이 타자.
수현: 그래. 저기 앉자.
지용: 너 이 버스 타고 다녀?
수현: 응. 너는 어디 가?
지용: 나는 홍대에서 친구 만나.
수현: 아, 이 버스 홍대 가지.
지용: 응. 나이 버스 자주 타. 자주 봐자.

coreVocab.meaning: to live, to stay, to spend (time)

grammar.title: Still + Present Perfect Continuous in the Negative
grammar.meaning: I still have not been able to / cannot do NOUN.
grammar.explanation: Ex) 아직 + 졸업 + 못 했어(요) → 아직 졸업 못 했어(요). = I still have not been able to graduate.

examples.english: I still have not been able to graduate.

exercises.english: I still have not been able to do homework.

pronunciation.rule: When a syllable ends with 찻 (which sounds the same as 닫 when in the 받침 position) and is followed by a syllable that begins with 브, the 브 is pronounced as 뻐.
When a syllable ends with 쌍 (which sounds the same as 닫 when in the 받침 position) and is followed by a syllable that begins with 드, the 드 is pronounced as 또.
